purpose: Map the cross-asset contagion mechanism between FX carry positioning and commodity markets for integrated risk monitoring.
components: CFTC positioning correlations: r=0.50-0.60 between commodity longs and JPY shorts indicate correlated speculative exposure
Carry unwind trigger: forced liquidation of carry positions triggers correlated commodity position unwinding
Commodity price decline amplifier: commodity weakness hits exporter currencies, triggering further carry losses
Local-currency cost curve stabilizer: producer-currency weakness caps commodity downside but perpetuates FX weakness
August 2024 as confirming episode of the full reflexive loop in action
Initial carry unwind triggers commodity-linked currency depreciation
Commodity price decline amplifies via reduced commodity demand expectations
FX depreciation feeds back to carry losses, creating reflexive loop
Credit cycle discriminator determines containment: no credit turn means contained (positioning adjustment), credit turn means self-reinforcing
August 2024 example: copper -8.4%, Brent -7.3%, AUD/USD -4.5% in 48 hours despite no physical fundamentals change
current_assessment: Loop is real and documented but the carry-equity correlation spike during stress is coincident, not leading — it cannot be used for prediction. The credit cycle discriminator is the operational bridge for determining whether the loop will be contained or self-reinforcing. Current credit conditions favor containment.
limitations: CFTC data covers only a fraction of total FX and commodity positioning
Correlation between commodity and FX positioning may reflect common cause (risk appetite) rather than direct cross-market linkage
Local-currency cost curve offset varies significantly by commodity and producer country
August 2024 episode was brief — longer episodes may propagate differently
Correlation spike is coincident, not predictive
Theoretical circularity between carry causing equity drawdowns and vice versa is unresolved
Limited precedent for full three-factor conjunction (low spare capacity + crowded carry + BoJ normalization)
2008 is closest analogue but with significant confounds
